items.summary: This infographic highlights video analysis as a key prerequisite for understanding muscle strain mechanisms in sport, by identifying qualitative and quantitative situational characteristics at or around the time of injury, which helps in developing effective prevention and rehabilitation strategies.
items.curatedReason: 这份信息图强调了视频分析在理解运动中肌肉拉伤机制方面的价值。当你需要深入分析复杂运动损伤时，考虑利用视频数据来识别损伤发生时的定性和定量特征，这有助于制定更精准的预防和康复策略。
items.titleZh: 信息图：运动中肌肉拉伤的视频分析——不同肌群的定性和定量情境特征
items.summaryZh: 这份信息图强调了视频分析在理解运动中肌肉拉伤机制的重要性，通过识别损伤发生时或前后的定性（如接触机制、损伤相关活动）和定量（如关节角度、损伤时机）情境特征，为制定有效的损伤预防和康复策略提供了关键前提。
items.curatedReasonEn: This infographic highlights the value of video analysis in understanding muscle strain mechanisms in sports. When analyzing complex sports injuries, consider using video data to identify qualitative and quantitative characteristics at the time of injury, which helps in developing more precise prevention and rehabilitation strategies.
items.limitation: 信息图本身并非原始研究，而是对已有概念和方法的概括，未提供具体研究数据和结论。
items.limitationEn: The infographic itself is not original research but a summary of existing concepts and methods, without specific research data or conclusions.

items.title: "My aim is to contribute to a meaningful life." Experiences of physiotherapists treating children and adolescents with cerebral palsy experiencing pain-a qualitative study.
items.summary: A qualitative study explored physiotherapists' experiences treating children with cerebral palsy (CP) experiencing pain. Eight interviews revealed three key themes: facilitating wellbeing through bodily practice, embracing body experiences as central to pain interpretation, and creating alliances based on safety and respect.
items.curatedReason: 本研究通过定性访谈，揭示了物理治疗师在处理脑瘫儿童疼痛时的心理和实践经验，强调了关注儿童整体福祉、以身体体验为中心理解和治疗疼痛，以及建立信任关系的重要性。
items.titleZh: “我的目标是让生活更有意义。”物理治疗师治疗脑瘫儿童疼痛的经验：一项定性研究
items.summaryZh: 一项定性研究探讨了物理治疗师治疗患有疼痛的脑瘫儿童的经验。八次访谈揭示了三个主题：通过身体治疗促进福祉，以身体体验为中心理解和治疗疼痛，以及基于安全、信任和尊重的医患关系。
items.curatedReasonEn: This qualitative study reveals physiotherapists' psychological and practical experiences when treating pain in children with cerebral palsy, emphasizing the importance of focusing on holistic well-being, understanding and treating pain through bodily experiences, and building trust.
items.limitation: 本研究为定性研究，样本量小（8名物理治疗师），结果可能不具备广泛代表性。
items.limitationEn: This qualitative study has a small sample size (8 physiotherapists), and its findings may not be broadly representative.

items.title: Early Access and Adherence to Physiotherapy in Children With Cerebral Palsy: Hospital-Based Study in Cameroon.
items.summary: A cross-sectional study in Cameroon examined factors related to early access and adherence to physiotherapy services among children with cerebral palsy (CP). Only 38.3% of 94 children accessed physiotherapy early (before 5 months) and showed good adherence (≥50% sessions completed).
items.curatedReason: 这项喀麦隆的研究揭示了当地脑瘫儿童早期接受物理治疗和依从性的障碍。虽然是特定区域数据，但能让你反思自己的临床实践如何能更好地消除障碍、提升依从性。
items.titleZh: 喀麦隆脑瘫儿童早期物理治疗的可及性和依从性：一项基于医院的研究
items.summaryZh: 一项喀麦隆的横断面研究调查了脑瘫儿童早期接受物理治疗和依从性的相关因素。在94名儿童中，仅有38.3%的儿童在5个月前开始物理治疗，且依从性良好（完成≥50%的治疗）。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study from Cameroon highlights barriers to early access and adherence to physiotherapy for children with cerebral palsy in the region. While specific to one area, it prompts reflection on how your own clinical practice can better remove barriers and improve adherence.
items.limitation: 本研究为横断面设计，无法建立因果关系，且数据来自单一三级转诊中心，可能存在选择偏倚，外部推广性有限。
items.limitationEn: This cross-sectional study cannot establish causality and is subject to selection bias due to data from a single tertiary referral center, limiting external generalizability.
items.journal: Pediatric physical therapy : the official publication of the Section on Pediatrics of the American Physical Therapy Association

items.title: The use of focus of attention for instruction and feedback during telehealth physiotherapy: An observational study.
items.summary: An exploratory observational study examined pediatric physiotherapists' use of focus of attention (FOA) when delivering instructions and feedback during telehealth for children with neurodevelopmental disorders. The study involved six physiotherapists each with two clients.
items.curatedReason: 这项观察性研究能让你了解在远程物理治疗中，如何通过指导和反馈的注意力焦点来优化儿童神经发育障碍的运动学习。它提供了实际实践中的洞察，对你的远程实践很有启发。
items.titleZh: 远程物理治疗中注意力焦点在指导和反馈中的应用：一项观察性研究
items.summaryZh: 一项探索性观察研究考察了儿科物理治疗师在通过远程医疗为神经发育障碍儿童提供指导和反馈时，如何利用注意力焦点。研究涉及六名物理治疗师，每人有两名患者。
items.curatedReasonEn: This observational study offers insights into optimizing motor learning for children with neurodevelopmental disorders in telerehabilitation, specifically through focus of attention in instruction and feedback. It provides practical insights that can inform your telerehabilitation practice.
items.limitation: 本研究为探索性观察性研究，样本量小（6名物理治疗师和12名患者），且结果基于特定地区的治疗中心，外推性受限。
items.limitationEn: This exploratory observational study has a small sample size (6 physiotherapists and 12 patients), and its results from specific regional therapy centers limit generalizability.

items.title: Validating an Activity Monitor for Acute Care Patients: The Patient/Resident Mobility Tracker (PREEMPT).
items.summary: The Patient/Resident Mobility Tracker (PREEMPT), an activity monitor for hospitalized individuals, was validated against video truth data. It demonstrated accuracy in measuring stride length, steps, stepping time, sedentary time, and activity time in 10 healthy older adults and 46 hospitalized patients.
items.curatedReason: PREEMPT 是一种用于住院患者的活动监测器，验证研究显示其在测量步态和活动方面具有良好的准确性。如果你在急性护理环境中工作，可以考虑将这类工具整合到你的患者评估和监测中，以便更客观地量化患者活动水平。
items.titleZh: 验证急性护理患者活动监测器：患者/居民移动追踪器 (PREEMPT)
items.summaryZh: 一项研究验证了用于住院患者的活动监测器 PREEMPT。该设备在10名健康老年人和46名住院患者中，通过与视频数据对比，在测量步长、步数、行走时间、静坐时间和活动时间方面表现出准确性。
items.curatedReasonEn: PREEMPT, an activity monitor for hospitalized patients, has shown good accuracy in gait and activity measurements during validation. If you work in acute care, consider integrating such tools into your patient assessment and monitoring to objectively quantify patient activity levels.
items.limitation: 研究样本中包含了健康老年人，这可能影响其在更广泛的急性住院患者群体中的适用性。
items.limitationEn: The study sample included healthy older adults, which may affect its applicability to a broader acute hospitalized patient population.
items.journal: Physiotherapy research international : the journal for researchers and clinicians in physical therapy

items.title: Exercise-based cancer telerehabilitation is safe but not superior to a single session of physiotherapy for improving quality of life: a randomised trial.
items.summary: An assessor-blinded, pragmatic randomized controlled trial found that 8-week, twice-weekly physiotherapist-led exercise-based cancer telerehabilitation (TeleCaRe) was safe but not superior to a single session of in-person assessment and advice for improving health-related quality of life in cancer survivors.
items.curatedReason: 这项随机对照试验表明，尽管基于运动的癌症远程康复是安全的，但它在改善生活质量方面并不优于单次面对面物理治疗。这意味着远程康复对癌症幸存者而言仍有价值，但可能需要更多干预来超越单次咨询的效果。
items.titleZh: 基于运动的癌症远程康复是安全的，但在改善生活质量方面并不优于单次物理治疗：一项随机试验
items.summaryZh: 一项评估者盲法、务实的随机对照试验发现，为期8周、每周两次由物理治疗师主导的基于运动的癌症远程康复（TeleCaRe）是安全的，但在改善癌症幸存者健康相关生活质量方面并不优于单次面对面评估和咨询。
items.curatedReasonEn: This randomized controlled trial indicates that while exercise-based cancer telerehabilitation is safe, it is not superior to a single session of in-person physiotherapy for improving quality of life. This suggests telerehabilitation remains valuable for cancer survivors but may require more intensive interventions to surpass the impact of a single consultation.
items.limitation: 研究的对照组是单次面对面咨询，而非持续性干预，可能限制了比较结果的临床意义。
items.limitationEn: The control group received a single in-person consultation rather than ongoing intervention, which may limit the clinical significance of the comparative results.

items.title: Individual Prognostication of Emergence from Post-Traumatic Amnesia: A Nationwide Cohort Study
items.summary: A nationwide cohort study aims to predict individual emergence from post-traumatic amnesia (PTA) in patients with moderate to severe traumatic brain injury (TBI).
items.curatedReason: 对于中重度TBI患者，这项观察研究提示我们，理解PTA恢复的个体差异是关键。PTA的预测模型仍是挑战，目前没有单一指标能完全取代临床判断。
items.titleZh: 创伤后失忆恢复的个体化预测：一项全国性队列研究
items.summaryZh: 一项全国性队列研究旨在预测中度至重度创伤性脑损伤（TBI）患者个体从创伤后失忆（PTA）中恢复的情况。
items.curatedReasonEn: For moderate to severe TBI patients, this observational study suggests that understanding individual variability in PTA emergence is key. Predicting PTA remains challenging, and no single metric can fully replace clinical judgment.
items.limitation: 作为一项队列研究，虽然规模较大，但仍需进一步研究以验证其预测模型的准确性和临床实用性。
items.limitationEn: As a cohort study, despite its large scale, further research is needed to validate the accuracy and clinical utility of its predictive model.
items.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation
